business.headline: EPF accounts to get 8.25% interest soon after government approvalThe government has approved an 8.25% EPF interest rate for 2025-26, paving the way for account credits this month. The decision lets EPFO begin processing payments for over 7 crore subscribers after Finance Ministry clearance.

The IPO that waited 10 years: Why NSE's listing is such a big dealFor nearly 10 years, the NSE IPO was the market's longest-running "coming soon" story. Now, India's biggest exchange is finally ready to test its value in public.

EPFO 3.0 may let you withdraw PF through UPI, ATM soon. Here's what could changeEPFO is preparing to launch its 3.0 platform with new digital withdrawal options for subscribers. The upgrade could speed up claims and simplify access to provident fund savings.

Why Trump said 'whatever' after new US Fed chief did not cut ratesUS President Donald Trump got a new Federal Reserve chief, but not the lower-rate signal he had been hoping for. What changed was his reaction.

Infosys, TCS, HCLTech down: Why are IT stocks falling after the recent rally?IT stocks fell at the opening bell. Among the biggest losers were Infosys, which fell 2.31%, Tech Mahindra was down 1.42%, HCLTech slid 1.17%, TCS lost 1.20%, while Persistent Systems and Wipro also lost 1.68% and 0.68% respectively in early trade.

Markets open flat after 4-day rally; IT stocks fall on US rate hike concernsThe S&P BSE Sensex was down 47.00 points, or 0.06%, at 77,108.62 in early trade, while the NSE Nifty50 managed to stay above the key 24,000 mark, rising 2.05 points to 24,087.75 as of 9:35 am.

Did China quietly save the world from an oil shock?As US-Iran tensions stirred oil fears, China cut imports and leaned on reserves and alternative energy. The move may have eased pressure on global supplies and limited a wider price surge.

NSE's long wait ends: Exchange files IPO papers, joins Jio in mega listing raceThe National Stock Exchange (NSE) has filed draft IPO papers after a delay stretching back to 2016. The move brings one of India's biggest listings closer as the primary market regains momentum.

India to allow 3.78 lakh UK car imports at lower duties under bilateral trade pactIndia will allow imports of 3.78 lakh conventional-engine passenger cars from the UK at concessional customs duty. The provision will apply for the first 15 years after the trade pact comes into force.

Reliance Jio denies Telegram founder's cyberattack allegationsReliance Jio has dismissed Telegram founder Pavel Durov's claim that it disrupted the messaging app's access outside India through Border Gateway Protocol (BGP) hijacking. The row comes as Telegram faces a temporary block in India over alleged links to the NEET paper leak case.

India-EU trade deal to be signed by end-2026, says Von der LeyenIn a bilateral meeting between Indian and EU delegations on the sidelines of the ongoing G7 Summit in France, the two sides also resolved to step up security and defence cooperation.

UK-India trade deal to take effect on July 15 despite dispute over steel tariffsUnder the deal, India will cut whisky tariffs to 40 per cent from 150 per cent, and automotives from 10% under a quota to 100 per cent, with Britain cutting tariffs on goods such as clothes, footwear and some food.

Dubai vs India: Which real estate market gives more bang for your buck?As property prices rise across Indian metros, investors are increasingly comparing Dubai with Mumbai, Delhi-NCR and Bengaluru. Larger homes, higher yields and tax-free returns are sharpening Dubai's appeal.

IT, PSU bank stocks lead gains as markets extend rally on falling oil pricesThe S&P BSE Sensex rose 347.14 points, or 0.45%, to settle at 77,155.62, while the NSE Nifty50 gained 96.55 points, or 0.40%, to close at 24,085.70.

IDBI Bank surges 19%: What's driving the sudden buying frenzy?IDBI Bank shares surged nearly 19% as renewed privatisation hopes and heavy trading lifted the stock. The rally reflects investor focus on the stalled stake sale, block deals and improving fundamentals.

SpaceX enters world's top 5 club: Elon Musk's company now worth more than AmazonSpaceX jumped past Amazon within days of its market debut after a sharp Wall Street rally. The surge, driven partly by options trading and index inclusion hopes, has intensified debate over its valuation.

Air India launches 'Basic' fare on domestic flights. What's included, what's notAir India has introduced a new Basic fare on select domestic routes that excludes complimentary meals. The pilot gives budget-conscious travellers a lower entry fare while leaving existing full-service fare options intact.

KPMG's AI-written report praising AI withdrawn after fake cases surfaceKPMG withdrew its global AI report after several organisations challenged fabricated case studies. The episode has sharpened concerns over AI hallucinations and the need for human verification.

A trade every 35 minutes? Trump's portfolio activity raises eyebrowsA financial disclosure showed US President Donald Trump's investment accounts made 3,642 trades in 90 days. The filing has prompted ethics questions, calls for an investigation and debate over conflicts of interest.

India isn't facing a 1991 crisis. But another challenge is taking shapeIndia has built a formidable forex cushion. But in an increasingly unpredictable world, will that be enough if foreign money starts drying up?

entertainment.headline: Spider-Man Brand New Day new trailer: An isolated Tom Holland battles Hulk The new trailer for Spider-Man: Brand New Day follows Peter Parker as he lives anonymously and faces a possible clash with the Hulk. It frames his isolation, unstable powers and trademark humour as the next chapter in his story.

Jai Jesus, Shree Allah: Ishaan Khatter's answer to religion question wins internet Ishaan Khatter gave a unique reply to a question about his religion during a recent AMA session. His answer resonated widely on social media, reflecting his belief in kindness and unity beyond religious boundaries.

Samantha, Raj Nidimoru visit Tirupati ahead of Maa Inti Bangaaram release. Watch Actor Samantha Ruth Prabhu visited the Tirumala Tirupati temple with Raj Nidimoru and BV Nandini Reddy before the release of Maa Inti Bangaaram. Photos and videos of their visit are now going viral on social media.

Main Vaapas..., Haunted, Bharat Bhhagya: Who's winning box office before Cocktail 2? Haunted 3D has moved ahead of Main Vaapas Aaunga and Bharat Bhhagya Viddhaata at the box office before Cocktail 2 arrives. The race also points to audiences responding strongly to horror-led theatrical experiences.

Dua Lipa, Callum Turner look smitten as they stroll Italy streets during honeymoon Dua Lipa and Callum Turner were seen enjoying romantic strolls through Italy during their honeymoon. But the destination choice carried a deeper significance for the newlyweds.

Kajal Aggarwal, Shreyas Talpade expose food adulteration in The India Story poster Kajal Aggarwal and Shreyas Talpade take on the issue of food adulteration in the newly unveiled poster of The India Story. The film promises an intense courtroom drama centred on a public health concern that affects millions across India.

Batwara 1947 teaser: Sunny Deol stands tall against hatred in Partition drama The teaser of Sunny Deol's Batwara 1947 revisits India's Independence and Partition through a man's fight against fear and hatred. The Rajkumar Santoshi directorial places courage and resilience at the centre of the story.

Animal gave Triptii different level of success: Maa Behen director Suresh Triveni Director Suresh Triveni, in an interview with India Today, spoke about Maa Behen's reception, Triptii Dimri's casting and the film's key symbols. He said the story was designed to entertain first, with its ideas landing quietly afterwards.

Venkatesh, Kalyan Ram's film goes on floors with puja ceremony. Full cast out Director Anil Ravipudi and actor Venkatesh Daggubati's new film was launched in Hyderabad, with Keerthy Suresh and Krithi Shetty confirmed as part of the cast. The project brings together established collaborators again and is being lined up for Sankranthi 2027.

Kalki 2898 AD director Nag Ashwin teases new update on the sequel's shooting Director Nag Ashwin discussed planning the shooting schedule for Kalki 2898 AD sequel, with most prep already complete. The update shifts attention to the sequel's production timeline and the pending Sum-80 casting announcement.

Revisiting Cocktail in 2026, film that punished Deepika's Veronica for being herself Homi Adjania's Cocktail, while celebrated for its music and performances, subtly declared Veronica the imperfect woman in the love story. As Cocktail 2 arrives, here's revisiting the 2012 film starring Deepika Padukone, Diana Penty and Saif Ali Khan.

Alia Bhatt shares why her 'filmy' daughter Raha Kapoor is meant for stage Actor Alia Bhatt opened up about daughter Raha Kapoor's growing interests, saying she is meant for the stage. She also reflected on her own childhood dream of becoming an actor.

Lilo & Stitch star Daveigh Chase's boyfriend shares cause of death Daveigh Chase's boyfriend has shared fresh details about the actor's final illness, shedding light on the health complications she faced before her death at 35.

Why aren't we talking enough about Suriya's deity transformation in Karuppu climax? Suriya's climactic transformation into Karuppaswamy in Karuppu delivered goosebumps, sent fans into a frenzy and proved to be far more than just another mass moment. As the film arrives on JioHotstar, it's worth revisiting the electrifying God-mode sequence that showcased the actor at his most intense, commanding and unforgettable.

Dhurandhar's script was given by PMO? Rakesh Bedi reacts to viral theory Was Dhurandhar's script really written by the PMO? Rakesh Bedi has finally reacted to the viral theory surrounding the blockbuster franchise. The actor also made an interesting revelation about the film's comic moments and his discussions with director Aditya Dhar.

Samantha admits bouncing off ideas with Raj Nidimoru: 99 per cent, I listen to him Actor-producer Samantha said she discusses nearly every decision with husband-storyteller Raj Nidimoru and sought his input throughout Maa Inti Bangaram. She said his influence runs from the script to post-production, underlining his central role at home and work.

Taylor Swift has a fix for Travis Kelce's picky eating in funny podcast cameo Singer Taylor Swift surprised fans at live New Heights taping with a playful cameo involving Travis Kelce. Find out why the crowd couldn't stop laughing.

Ravi Kishan's yoga video becomes meme gold, internet invents 'Gutkhasan' Ravi Kishan's yoga session at Gorakhpur grabbed attention for an unexpected reason after social media users claimed he was chewing something while performing asanas. The viral video sparked a flood of jokes and witty reactions across social media.

Yami Gautam, Aditya Dhar visit Ujjain's Mahakaleshwar temple with son and family Actor Yami Gautam and director Aditya Dhar visited Ujjain's Mahakaleshwar temple with their son, Vedavid, and relatives. Yami said the family felt blessed after darshan and praised the shrine's arrangements for devotees.

general news.headline: Midnight flights to Delhi, fear of Congress merger: Inside Sena UBT MPs' revoltSix Sena UBT MPs quietly reached Delhi from various destinations shortly after midnight on June 16, met Eknath Shinde and submitted a letter to the Lok Sabha Speaker. A second split in Uddhav Thackeray's Sena faction is almost inevitable.

Pak plans to deploy submarines in Bay of Bengal, a first since 1971 bloody noseEven as Pakistan's China-built Hangor-class submarine arrived in Karachi, senior naval officers spoke of using it to maintain a presence in the Bay of Bengal. The ambition is significant because Pakistan has had little meaningful naval presence in the Bay of Bengal since its defeat in the 1971 war and the creation of Bangladesh.

Shashi Tharoor's driver salary analogy to counter Andhra CM's delimitation pitchThe Congress MP hit back at N Chandrababu Naidu over his criticism of the opposition after the Constitution amendment bill was defeated in Parliament. He said southern chief ministers were worried about losing political weight.

Netanyahu stares at his darkest hour as Trump makes peace with IranDonald Trump and Iran's President signed an initial deal to end the war. However, the agreement has left Benjamin Netanyahu politically exposed as the deal failed to meet a single objective for which Israel pursued the Iran war.

India-born Balwinder became Jaswinder for US citizenship. This happened nextAn India-born man, Balwinder Singh, who changed his name to Jaswinder Singh to obtain immigration benefits and eventually US citizenship, is facing a civil lawsuit. The federal complaint seeks to revoke Singh's naturalisation, claiming he obtained US citizenship through deception and misrepresentation.

Live: 6 rebel Sena UBT MPs may form new faction, Sanjay Bandu Jadhav to leadShiv Sena UBT Crisis Live Updates: Shiv Sena (UBT) held a crucial parliamentary party meeting in the Parliament House on Thursday amid intense speculation that a majority of its Lok Sabha MPs could cross over to the Eknath Shinde-led Shiv Sena. The developments are being closely watched as the biggest test yet of Uddhav Thackeray's grip over the party's parliamentary wing following reports that six of the outfit's nine Lok Sabha MPs are in touch with the rival camp. Despite being informed about the 11 am meeting, only three Lok Sabha MPs — Anil Desai, Arvind Sawant and Rajabhau Waje — had attended by the time proceedings began. Rajya Sabha MP and senior party leader Sanjay Raut was also present. Later, sources told India Today that the MPs absent from the meet could form a separate faction, with Parbhani MP Sanjay Bandu Jadhav likely to lead it. Follow IndiaToday.in for the latest updates.

Only 3 Indian institutes make the top 200 in QS World University Rankings 2027The QS World University Rankings 2027 place IIT Delhi as India's top-ranked university at 118th globally, up five places from last year. Only three Indian institutions, IIT Delhi, IIT Bombay, and IIT Madras, secured spots among the world's top 200 universities.

Monsoon struggling: Rain deficit jumps to 42%, recovery looks difficultIndia's southwest monsoon has slowed sharply, pushing the national rain deficit to 42%. Weak clouding over core monsoon regions has made an early recovery harder.

A Versailles deal in 2026 and US's total surrenderMore than a century after US President Woodrow Wilson helped shape the Treaty of Versailles, Donald Trump signed a 14-point accord with Iran at the same palace. While Wilson projected American dominance, critics argue the new deal marks a reversal, with Washington making greater concessions than Tehran.

Cristiano Ronaldo's selfishness hurt Portugal: Henry's verdict on DR Congo drawFIFA World Cup 2026: Thierry Henry criticised Cristiano Ronaldo after Portugal's 1-1 draw with DR Congo in Houston. His remarks sharpened the debate over Ronaldo's individual pursuit and Portugal's team balance.

Trump, Pezeshkian digitally sign US-Iran MoU to end conflict, reopen Hormuz Live UpdatesUS President Donald Trump and Iranian President Masoud Pezeshkian digitally signed a memorandum of understanding to end the conflict and reopen the Strait of Hormuz. The deal includes sanctions relief, resumed Iranian oil exports, talks on implementation, and a 60-day framework for negotiating a broader agreement.

Ram Temple donation case: SIT finds CCTV tampering clues, security staff under probeThe SIT has found clues of possible CCTV tampering in the Ram Mandir donation theft probe. According to documents examined by the SIT, nearly Rs 10 crore was spent on security arrangements at the temple complex over the past 11 months.

Bengaluru man spots 40 people waiting for Uber while Ola cabs sit idle, shares reasonA LinkedIn post on passengers waiting for Uber over readily available Ola cabs at Bengaluru airport sparked debate online. The discussion centred on whether familiarity and trust matter more to consumers than price or convenience.

Tamil anthem sung 1st in Assembly, reversing order that sparked Vijay oath rowAt Thursday's Assembly session, the Tamil anthem was rendered first before proceedings commenced, while the National Anthem was played later in accordance with the convention followed in the state for decades.

Dhurandhar's script was given by PMO? Rakesh Bedi reacts to viral theoryWas Dhurandhar's script really written by the PMO? Rakesh Bedi has finally reacted to the viral theory surrounding the blockbuster franchise. The actor also made an interesting revelation about the film's comic moments and his discussions with director Aditya Dhar.

The IPO that waited 10 years: Why NSE's listing is such a big dealFor nearly 10 years, the NSE IPO was the market's longest-running "coming soon" story. Now, India's biggest exchange is finally ready to test its value in public.

Did China quietly save the world from an oil shock?As US-Iran tensions stirred oil fears, China cut imports and leaned on reserves and alternative energy. The move may have eased pressure on global supplies and limited a wider price surge.

Roti or rice for diabetes? Expert explains the better choiceFor years, Indians have debated whether rice or roti is the healthier choice for people with diabetes. According to leading diabetologist Dr. Anoop Mishra, the answer is more nuanced than simply choosing one over the other.

Cold-blooded: Sikh elderly couple shot dead in Pak gurudwara, assailants on the runAn elderly Sikh couple was gunned down in a gurudwara in Pakistan's Khyber Pakhtunkhwa. Reacting to it, the Akal Takht and the BJP condemned the killing. The BJP said that the killing is a "grim reminder of the insecurity, fear, and persecution faced by religious minorities in Pakistan".

3 loyalists, 6 rebels: New Sena faction set to emerge after Uddhav meeting flop Live UpdatesOnly three of Shiv Sena (UBT)'s nine Lok Sabha MPs attended a crucial Delhi meeting. The absence of six MPs intensified talk of a fresh split and appeared to match rebel claims of two-thirds support.

Revisiting Cocktail in 2026, film that punished Deepika's Veronica for being herselfHomi Adjania's Cocktail, while celebrated for its music and performances, subtly declared Veronica the imperfect woman in the love story. As Cocktail 2 arrives, here's revisiting the 2012 film starring Deepika Padukone, Diana Penty and Saif Ali Khan.

Dowry abuse, CCTV surveillance: Woman dies by suicide only 47 days into marriageVictim Vishakha Tilkar married Dr Nitin Tilkar on May 30. Their relationship during the initial days was cordial, but gradually Vishakha was subjected to alleged physical and mental assault.

Monsoon has hit a wall: Here's what needs to change for rain to returnIndia's monsoon advance has stalled for nearly two weeks after an early onset. Forecasts suggest weakening dry air and stronger winds could restart its spread in the coming days.

Summers too hot for cricket? BCCI considers starting IPL 2027 in early MarchThe BCCI is considering moving the IPL to a March 10 to May 15 window to avoid late-season heat and pre-monsoon rain. The plan keeps the tournament at 74 matches for now and would require domestic cricket to finish earlier.

sports.headline: Hiding behind Cristiano Ronaldo? Bruno Fernandes' DR Congo no-show raises questions Portugal's 1-1 draw with DR Congo reopened the debate over Cristiano Ronaldo after another scoreless night. But the backlash also exposed wider attacking failures, with Bruno Fernandes and Portugal's creators unable to supply the final pass.

I think Ishan Kishan has already got his South African visa: Sunil Gavaskar Sunil Gavaskar said Ishan Kishan's 125 against Afghanistan has nearly sealed his place in India's 2027 ODI plans. He and Graeme Swann praised Kishan's improved range, flexibility and growing authority.

Ronaldo's fear factor fading? DR Congo defender's candid remarks raise eyebrows Cristiano Ronaldo's standing as Portugal's main attacking threat came under the spotlight after DR Congo midfielder Ngal'ayel Mukau revealed his side had no special plan to stop the veteran forward in their FIFA World Cup 2026 clash.

Summers too hot for cricket? BCCI considers starting IPL 2027 in early March The BCCI is considering moving the IPL to a March 10 to May 15 window to avoid late-season heat and pre-monsoon rain. The plan keeps the tournament at 74 matches for now and would require domestic cricket to finish earlier.

Sub Cristiano Ronaldo when I need goals? Portugal coach laments doubters FIFA World Cup 2026: Portugal opened with a 1-1 draw against DR Congo as Cristiano Ronaldo made his sixth World Cup appearance. The result sharpened doubts over their attacking balance and their continuing dependence on a 41-year-old captain.

Watch: Stylish Phillips dons sunglasses to survive Archer barrage at The Oval Glenn Phillips produced one of the most memorable moments of the opening day of the second Test at The Oval when he called for a pair of sunglasses to deal with the glare from the sightscreen before facing a fierce spell from Jofra Archer.

Brar credits domestic cricket and India A stint for strong international start Gurnoor Brar has announced himself in the Afghanistan ODI series with pace, bounce and six wickets in two matches. His seamless jump from Ranji and India A underlines how India's system keeps producing fast bowlers ready for international cricket.

Luis Diaz sparks Colombia to commanding 3-1 win over debutants Uzbekistan Luis Diaz starred with a goal and an assist as Colombia kicked off their FIFA World Cup 2026 campaign with a commanding 3-1 win over tournament debutants Uzbekistan in Group K at a packed Azteca Stadium on Wednesday.

Messi 3-0 Ronaldo after World Cup Round 1: Did we not see it coming? FIFA World Cup 2026: Lionel Messi arrived at the World Cup carrying a legacy and somehow made it bigger. Cristiano Ronaldo arrived chasing history, but after Round 1, the tournament feels less like a race between rivals and more like a tale of two timelines.

Mexican military shoots down drone near South Korea's training camp Mexican military personnel neutralised an unauthorised drone near South Korea's training base in Guadalajara ahead of the team's Group A clash against hosts Mexico at the FIFA World Cup 2026.

India's Women's honeymoon ends, the real test begins right now India have made a strong start to their Women's T20 World Cup campaign with wins over Pakistan and the Netherlands, going top of Group A on net run rate, but tougher challenges now await against South Africa and Australia.

Hamilton reborn, Mercedes exposed: 5 key takeaways from Monaco and SpainJust a few races ago, Mercedes looked untouchable. Monaco and Barcelona have changed everything. From Lewis Hamilton's resurgence to Mercedes' growing troubles, here are five key takeaways from a dramatic double-header.

Cristiano Ronaldo's selfishness hurt Portugal: Henry's verdict on DR Congo drawFIFA World Cup 2026: Thierry Henry criticised Cristiano Ronaldo after Portugal's 1-1 draw with DR Congo in Houston. His remarks sharpened the debate over Ronaldo's individual pursuit and Portugal's team balance.

Ghana snatch dramatic stoppage-time win over PanamaCaleb Yirenkyi's stoppage-time strike earned Ghana a dramatic 1-0 win over Panama in a lively World Cup 2026 Group L clash in Toronto on Wednesday. Ghana secured all three points despite losing goalkeeper Lawrence Ati Zigi to injury at half-time.

Kane credits Tuchel's half-time pep talk for England's 4-2 victory over CroatiaEngland captain Harry Kane credited Thomas Tuchel's half-time intervention for transforming his side's performance as the Three Lions overcame Croatia 4-2 in a thrilling FIFA World Cup opener.

After Vozinha's heroics, his mother secures US visa to watch Cape VerdeU.S. officials have moved to secure a visa for Cape Verde goalkeeper Vozinha's mother before Sunday's Uruguay match. The breakthrough follows his standout World Cup display against Spain and sets up an emotional reunion in Miami.

'Messi, Messi' chants rain down on Ronaldo as Portugal held by DR CongoFIFA World Cup 2026: Portugal opened their World Cup campaign with a 1-1 draw against DR Congo as Cristiano Ronaldo again failed to score. The result and the Messi chants around him sharpened the early contrast between football's two long-time rivals.

Thomas Tuchel gets it right as England overturn Croatia to win World Cup openerFIFA World Cup 2026: Thomas Tuchel's halftime changes proved decisive as England recovered from a sluggish first half to beat Croatia 4-2 in their World Cup opener, with Jude Bellingham leading the fightback.

UZB 1 - 3 COL Highlights: Luis Diaz inspires Colombia to overcome wasteful Uzbekistan in Group KUzbekistan (UZB) vs Colombia (COL) Highlights, FIFA World Cup 2026: Luis Diaz inspired Colombia to a commanding win over Uzbekistan, as they put three past the debutants from Asia. After assisting Daniel Munoz for the first goal, Diaz scored the second to put Colombia back in front. Jaminton Campaz scored the third in the last second of the injury time to seal the deal in New Mexico City.

Age catches up? Cristiano Ronaldo blanks as Portugal held 1-1 by DR CongoFIFA World Cup 2026: Cristiano Ronaldo's record-breaking World Cup dream remains on hold after a frustrating night in Houston. Portugal may have escaped with a point against DR Congo, but questions over their creativity and dependence on a 41-year-old icon are only growing louder.

How Shubman Gill battled cramps, Lucknow heat for heroic Afghanistan centuryIND vs AFG, 2nd ODI: Shubman Gill overcame cramps and the intense Lucknow heat to smash a brilliant 154 off 110 balls, leading India to a 170-run win over Afghanistan and an unassailable 2-0 series lead.

India Women hammer Netherlands, leapfrog Australia to go top of tableWomen's T20 World Cup, IND vs NED: India hammered the Netherlands by 95 runs in Leeds on Wednesday to climb to the top of Group A, overtaking Australia on net run rate.

In oppressive Lucknow heat, Shubman Gill leads India to his 1st series winIndia captain Shubman Gill won his first ODI series as a captain after India took an unassailable 2-0 lead against Afghanistan after the 2nd game of the series. Shubman led from the front with a terrific hundred in the oppressive heat in Lucknow.

Shreyanka Patil suffers freak foot injury, stretchered off from park against NetherlandsIndia suffered a major injury scare during their Women's T20 World Cup clash against the Netherlands after Shreyanka Patil twisted her right ankle while fielding and had to be stretchered off the ground at Headingley.

World Cup Highlights: Ronaldo disappoints as Congo take a point in 1-1 drawPortugal (POR) vs DR Congo (COD) Highlights, FIFA World Cup 2026: (POR 1-1 COD). DR Congo have taken a point of the mighty Portugal here in Houston. Cristiano Ronaldo had a poor outing having been less involved in the game as it went deeper.

Vaibhav Sooryavanshi pulls off stunning acrobatic catch vs Afghanistan A: WatchIND A vs AFG A: Vaibhav Sooryavanshi produced a stunning acrobatic catch and followed it up with a brisk 38-run knock as India A thrashed Afghanistan A by 101 runs in the Tri-Nation Series in Dambulla.

Vozinha now has Instagram followers 22 times his country's populationThe FIFA World Cup has the ability to change the life of any footballer in the world. Cape Verde goalkeeper Vozinha got to know that first hand after his Instagram following became 22 times of his country's population.

Dambulla tri-series: Tilak Varma and India A make amends, storm into the finalIndia A vs Afghanistan A: Tilak Varma struck 59 and led India A to a 101-run win over Afghanistan A in Dambulla. The result sealed a tri-series final spot after criticism followed the chaotic loss to Sri Lanka A.

Australia Women keep India on their toes, storm past Bangladesh with easeWomen's T20 World Cup, AUS vs BAN: Australia continued their impressive run in the tournament with a dominant nine-wicket win over Bangladesh, strengthening their position at the top of the group and piling pressure on India heading into the remaining matches.

education.headline: The role of data analytics in modern advertising educationThis transformation also exercises a significant impact on the realm of 'advertising education.'

NCERT deletes Azad Pak, adds China's aggression reference in Class 12 textbookThe NCERT has made final changes to its Class 12 Political Science textbook syllabus for the upcoming academic year, including a revision in reference to India's border situation with China, Azad Pakistan, Article 370 and more.

NEET row: Bihar cops find 6 post-dated cheques 'issued for question paper brokers'Six post-dated cheques which were suspected to have been issued in favour of mafia demanding over Rs 30 lakh from each candidate seeking alleged leaked question paper ahead of NEET held last month were recovered by Bihar Police.

Forest fires surge tenfold from 2004 to 2017: Causes, impact and preventionAmidst a relentless surge, intense forest fires are now multiplying tenfold, casting a menacing shadow over our flora and fauna. Year by year, their fiery grip tightens, threatening ecosystems far and wide, now extending their reach to impact human lives.

Fresh plea in Supreme Court to recall NEET results, reconduct examsA fresh petition has been filed in the Supreme Court to recall the National Entrance cum Eligibility Test - Under Graduate (NEET-UG) 2024 results and re-conduct fresh examination after discrepancies in the declared results.

NTA issues clarification after NEET result controversy over toppers, high cut-offIn a detailed statement, the National Testing Agency (NTA) said that the increase in cut-off and number of toppers in the NEET-UG reflect the competitive nature of the exam and maintained that the test's integrity has not been compromised.

Exclusive: Arvind Kejriwal claims 'BJP succession war, PM backing Amit Shah'Speaking exclusively to India Today TV, Delhi Chief Minister Arvind Kejriwal claimed that there is a "succession war" within the BJP. He also claimed that the ruling party is "not fighting the Lok Sabha elections as a team".

4 medical students arrested in Bihar for appearing as proxies for NEET aspirantsFour MBBS students who appeared in place of NEET aspirants in the medical entrance exam were detained in Bihar's Purnea.

NEET-UG question paper not leaked, claims baseless: National Testing AgencyThe reports claiming question paper leak in the medical entrance exam NEET-UG are "completely baseless and without any ground", the National Testing Agency (NTA) has clarified.
The generation with answers: Tech-savvy Indian youth tackling global challengesIndia's tech-savvy youth rewriting the global narrative, one solution at a time.

Left sweeps JNU students' polls, Dhananjay is first Dalit presidentThe Left swept the Jawaharlal Nehru University Students' Union (JNUSU) polls, winning all four posts and trouncing the RSS-affiliated ABVP. The ABVP was leading at one point, but the Left eventually surged ahead and emerged victorious.
The power of knowledge: Education's influence on real estate market trendsIn a dynamic sector like real estate, investing in continuing education is not simply an option; it is a requirement.

JNU students' body election on March 22 after 4 yearsThe Jawaharlal Nehru University Students' Union (JNUSU) polls will take place on March 22, more than four years after the last elections were held in September 2019.

UP 12th board exam math, biology papers leaked on WhatsApp, complaint filedThe mathematics and biology question papers for the Uttar Pradesh class 12 board exam were allegedly leaked on a WhatsApp group.

UP Police Constable recruitment exam cancelled days after paper leakThe Uttar Pradesh Police Constable recruitment exam held on February 17 and 18 was canceled due to a paper leak. The Chief Minister has announced a re-examination within the next six months.
